title: Is it possible to extract the body of Email to Monday column?
excerpt: Hi! I would like to know, is there any automation that could possibly extract the name, email and date automatically from Email to monday’s column board? If so, what is the step to do the automation?

posts.author: Rein (Rein)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi! I would like to know, is there any automation that could possibly extract the name, email and date automatically from Email to monday’s column board? If so, what is the step to do the automation?

posts.author: Nir-Jetpack (Nir @ Jetpack Team)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Rein Extract marketplace app (see link below) extracts content from board updates. Currently we extract emails and sender names, and adds them to your board items so you can automate your CRM and helpdesk workflows without the need to manually copy and paste data.

Can you share more about your usecase so we can consider extracting the date?
Thanks!

monday.com

monday.com: Apps Marketplace

monday.com apps marketplace, Expand the capabilities of your Work OS with monday apps

posts.author: NoyNoy (Noy Malka)
posts.cookedHtml: Him, you know if there is a option to extract also the CC and BCC from the email?

posts.author: Urusa (Urusa Siddiqui)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @Rein
You can do it by using the AI columns you can use the AI column named extract info then extract name, email id and date from the update.

First you need to create an item when email is received in the group then it will copy the whole mail in the updates from there you can extract your info.

I hope this will work.

posts.author: Mariia (Mariia Mykhasiuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @Rein ! If the goal is to turn incoming emails into structured items, Email to Task is worth a look. It automatically converts emails into items and maps things like sender email, subject, body, and received date into dedicated columns, so you don’t have to extract or copy anything manually.

There’s a 14-day trial, and happy to walk through the setup on a quick demo if helpful.
